other_observers: None; I was the original observer.
specific_location: At the eastern end of CR E in the smaller leafless trees between the black locusts and the much taller cottonwoods nearer the reservoir.
description: I immediately recognized this bird as a Blue-winged Warbler. The front of the head, throat, chest, and belly were all bright yellow. The eye was black and the lore was very dark gray and there was a short dark post ocular mark. The auriculars were a golden olive, as was the back of the crown and nape which extended to the upper back. The undertail coverts were white as was the majority of the undertail. The wings were a bluish-gray with two distinct white wing bars. The bill was blackish-brown and sharply pointed; the lower mandible appeared lighter colored. The legs were dark. The upper tail was dark. The rump was not seen.Behaviors: The bird was feeding low - about four feet above the ground - in the leafless trees immediately adjacent to the locust trees at Neenoshe Reservoir. It was perched for about ten seconds before it flew. My view was almost a full side view with the bird turned slightly toward me.Call: Not heardPlumage: Adult female probably
